candidate_summary: TWIN-PLANE-HEAD ties K transformer head pairs by Householder mirror reflection W_B = (I - 2 n n^T) W_A across a fixed random non-symmetry plane n per pair.

results_analysis.title: Efficient Adaptation of Pre-trained Vision Transformer via Householder Transformation
results_analysis.reason: PAID applies Householder reflection (I - 2vv^T) to transformer weight direction — the exact same operator as TWIN-PLANE-HEAD's mirror tie; semantic and functional thresholds both exceeded.

results_analysis.title: Head-wise Shareable Attention for Large Language Models (EMNLP 2024)
results_analysis.reason: Head-wise weight sharing across LLM attention heads — same head-pair tying artefact, differing only by identity-sharing vs Householder reflection; passes semantic and functional thresholds.

results_analysis.title: Head-wise Shareable Attention for LLMs (arxiv 2402.11819)
results_analysis.reason: Arxiv companion of rank 2 — same head-parameter-sharing artefact across heads; passes semantic and functional thresholds.

results_analysis.title: Rethinking Attention Output Projection: Structured (2026/03)
results_analysis.reason: Walsh-Hadamard fixed parameter-free orthogonal transform couples head outputs — same fixed-orthogonal-transform-across-head-pairs pattern as TWIN-PLANE-HEAD; both sem and func above 0.7.

results_analysis.title: LESTD: LLM Compression via Learning-Based Subspace Tensor Decomposition
results_analysis.reason: Shared low-rank orthonormal subspace across heads — coupling/sharing of head parameters via orthonormal structure; meets thresholds.

results_analysis.title: Vision Transformers with Hierarchical Attention
results_analysis.reason: Tree-structured hierarchical attention — unrelated to head-pair Householder mirror tying.

results_analysis.title: A Visual Guide to Attention Variants in Modern LLMs
results_analysis.reason: GQA/MQA/MLA survey — key sharing only, not weight-matrix Householder mirror tying.

results_analysis.title: Multi-Head Attention Architecture Gallery
results_analysis.reason: Baseline MHA description — no twinning or reflection mechanism present.

verdict_sentence: Five distinct results (PAID Householder transformer adaptation, two Head-wise Shareable Attention papers, Walsh-Hadamard head-coupling, LESTD shared orthonormal subspace) each independently clear both the semantic (>=0.7) and functional (>=0.7) thresholds, with PAID using the exact Householder mirror operator on transformer weights — TWIN-PLANE-HEAD lacks novelty.
